description:

title: The Voyage of the Alaska Union w/ Mr. White Keys: Wasilla Speaker Series
description:
Where: Mat-Su Health Foundation
When: Doors open at 6:30PM, Presentation starts at 7PM. Event usually lasts about an hour.
Free to attend, no need to register
Presentation details: Eighty Greenhorns from Chicago set out to strike it rich in Alaska in 1898. It was the largest Gold Rush expedition ever launched, and they had no idea what they were in for. One of the prospectors, Charles Harris, toted a 40-pound camera with him during the entire odyssey, but his images have remained unseen for 120 years.
In The Voyage of The Alaska Union, Mr. Whitekeys reveals a true tale of Alaska History that has never been told. Lavishly illustrated with 100 of Charles Harris’s photographs, the book relies on century-old diaries, letters, manuscripts, and newspaper accounts that tell the story in the words of the men who were there on the Koyukuk in 1898.
It’s the story of tough men who survived with what they had. It may be 70 below, you may have forgotten to pack your tent on a hundred-mile trek, but if you had doughnuts, everything was just fine.

title: Winter Ladies’ Only Outing (Cold Weather Kayaking)
description:
Come join us for a Ladies’ Only Cold-Weather Kayaking Adventure!
Ladies, let’s embrace the crisp air, stunning winter light, and pure Alaska magic from the water! We’ll kick things off with a quick safety briefing at the Eklutna Tailrace—then we’re launching into a one-of-a-kind kayaking experience that’s peaceful, powerful, and absolutely unforgettable.
We’ve got you covered with a warming area, steaming hot cocoa, and fresh pastries. Your local, experienced guides will be right by your side, making sure you feel confident and inspired the entire time.
Can’t make this month’s ladies-only adventure?
No worries—more chances to get outside together are on the way:
Future monthly adventures: Stay tuned for releases!
Adventure, connection, and some serious cold-weather magic—let’s make it happen!

title: Training | Positive and Adverse Childhood Experiences (Online Only)
description:
This FREE presentation is open to ADULTS who are interested in learning how to identify and counteract Adverse Childhood Experiences (ACEs) to develop resilient kids.
Registration is not required.
Frequent or prolonged exposure to stress can create toxic stress which can damage the developing brain of a child and overall health. A Survival Mode Response to toxic stress increases a child’s heart rate, blood pressure, breathing and muscle tension. Their thinking brain is knocked offline. Self-protection is their priority. In other words: “I can’t hear you! I can’t respond to you! I am just trying to be safe!”
Learn How Resilience Trumps ACEs! Parents, Teachers and Caregivers can help by:
– Gaining an understanding of ACEs and how they affect children’s social, emotional, and cognitive abilities and their connection a wide range of health problems throughout a person’s lifespan.
– Creating environments where children feel safe emotionally & physically.
– Helping children identify feelings & manage emotions
– Creating a safe physical & emotional environment at home, in school, and in neighborhoods.
– Share ways to build resilience.
Questions?
Presentation Content Contact: boydenmichelle@gmail.com
Event Details Contact: candrews@rockmatsu.org
Zoom Info: https://us06web.zoom.us/j/82576480185
Passcode: 288902

title: Ball in the House: R&B, Soul, Pop acapella
description:
Join the Palmer Arts Council and Alaska Junior Theater for a night of amazing a capella music! Ball in the House is an R&B/Soul/Pop a cappella group based out of Boston, MA, whose high-energy shows have audiences singing, dancing, and even beatboxing along!
With their extensive tour schedule (averaging 200 shows a year), Ball in the House has performed everywhere – from theaters to performing arts centers, fairs & festivals, to opening for some of the biggest acts in the world, such as The Beach Boys, The Jonas Brothers, The Temptations, Fantasia, Gladys Knight, Lionel Richie, Jessica Simpson, Blondie, Smokey Robinson, KC & The Sunshine Band, Diamond Rio, Colin Raye, Orleans, and numerous others.
They headlined the 2018 & 2016 China International Chorus Festival in Beijing, won the 2016 Boston Harmony Sweepstakes, and were voted 2014 APCA Band of the Year and 2009 Best of Boston for Outstanding Musical Performers. For six years, they were the voices behind all the Cool Whip commercials and recently wrote and performed the jingle for Amazon’s Prime Day. Ball in the House has appeared on America’s Got Talent, The TODAY Show, The Daily Buzz, the Philadelphia 4th of July Parade, and SBS Culture Club and MBC News in Seoul, South Korea.
Beyond the stage, the group is dedicated to music education, offering workshops and school performances that inspire and engage the next generation of musicians. With a fresh take on classic sounds and a passion for connecting with audiences, Ball in the House proves that the voice is the most powerful instrument of all.
Ball in the House consists of the talents of Oscar Hall (Baritone), Tyler Nordin (Tenor), Kevin Cincotta-Guest (Bass), Wallace Thomas (High Tenor) and Jon Ryan (Vocal Percussion).
Ticket Info:General Admission- $25PAC Members- $20Students age 18 and younger- $15Purchase tickets here:

title: Meadow Lakes Community Health Fair
description:
New to Health Fairs?
Please review this one-pager to learn what to expect at a health fair.
We Make It Easy to Stay on Top of Your Numbers
Alaska Health Fair, Inc., a nonprofit, has been providing health education and affordable blood screenings since 1980. Each year, thousands of Alaskans take advantage of the affordable blood tests offered at health fairs. We collaborate with the largest clinical laboratory in the nation to ensure the availability of high-quality blood screenings to all Alaskans at a nonprofit price.
Brochure Screenings & Affordable Blood
Wellness the Alaskan Way
From the North Slope to Southeast, health fairs bring neighbors together to help neighbors take charge of their health through volunteering, education and preventative screenings. Our fairs unite hundreds of local medical and non-medical volunteers with health-focused organizations who share their expertise at no cost. Exhibitor space is always free at AHF events! This grassroots model of Alaskans supporting Alaskans has been building a stronger, healthier state.
Would You Like to Host a Health Fair?
Our incredible team makes hosting a health fair a breeze! We’ll provide comprehensive assistance at every stage to ensure your event’s success. Fill out this quick form, and we’ll contact you with the details.

title: Strength Made Simple
description:
Are you… Feeling overwhelmed in the gym? Are you a fitness beginner? Plateaued in your training? Seeking confidence in your program?
This is for you!!! This workshop includes foundational education on exercise form, proper warm up, and cool down, a nutritional blueprint and a training program to take home. Be prepared to lift, move and sweat! Let’s make strength simple.

title: Stellar Spaghetti Cook Off Fundraiser
description:
🚀🍝 STELLAR SPAGHETTI COOK OFF LAUNCHING! 🍝🚀
Prepare for launch! The Stellar Spaghetti Cook Off is touching down on March 21st at Denali Harley-Davidson from 12PM–3PM, and it’s going to be out of this world. 🌌
Local cooks will battle it out for galactic glory, serving up their best spaghetti creations while you help decide which sauce reigns supreme. Entry fees and voting fees are all donations to Katie’s Handprint Charity, the official charity of Spenard Bike Week, so every bite supports an amazing cause. ❤️
🌟 KEY DRAWING AT 3PM! 🌟
Stick around until the end — you never know when luck will align like the planets.
👨‍👩‍👧‍👦 Fun for the whole family
👩‍🚀 Dress in your best space costume for a chance to win a grand prize
🍝 Cosmic carbs, saucy competition, and stellar vibes
Bring your crew, fuel up on spaghetti, and help us kick off Spenard Bike Week season in true space-themed style. One small step for pasta… one giant leap for Bike Week. 👽✨
📅 March 21
📍 Denali Harley-Davidson
⏰ 12PM–3PM
🍝 Spaghetti Cook Off
🗝️ Key Drawing at 3PM
🚀 Space Costumes Encouraged
This event is going full throttle to the stars — don’t miss it!
$20 to enter your spaghetti – donation to Katie’s Handprint
$5 to taste and vote- donation to Katie’s Handprint
Please Register In Advance to bring Spaghetti! -email marketing@ harleyalaska.com or stop into Denali H-D to sign up! Spaghetti must be prepped, hot and ready to serve onsite in a crockpot by 12PM. Please bring an extension cord!
Winner determined by popular vote, bring your friends and family!

title: Palmer : Mountain Brew Hand Embroidery Class
description:
The Mountain Brew design by Landly AK is the perfect introduction to hand embroidery. In this class you will learn 6 stitches in a 4-hour period. All supplies needed will be provided but feel to bring any of your own things if you prefer. I recommend you bring glasses that you wear, even occasionally.You will go home with your piece, 5-inch hoop, size 5 needle and thread needed to complete any leftover work at home. This class is for 21 and older, a drink will be included.Please keep in mind that embroidery is a slower craft and that it is likely you do not finish the piece before class is over.If you are unable to register online and would like to attend, please message me so I can plan for your attendance. No refunds but if you are unable to attend class after signing up, we can move you to a later class within the year of 2026, as long as you reach out to me any time before class starts.
Bleeding Heart Brewery 562 S. Denali St. Palmer AK

title: 24th Annual Spaghetti Dinner & Auction
description:
🌟 Join us for a night filled with pasta, fun, laughter, and community love! 🌟
We’re excited to welcome you to the 24th Annual Spaghetti Dinner & Auction benefiting Special Olympics Alaska Mat-Su Community Program!
📅 Saturday, March 21st
📍 Mat-Su Career & Tech High School
⏰ Doors open at 4:30 PM
🍽️ Dinner served 5:00–7:00 PM
🎉 Our one-of-a-kind $1 Auction runs 6:30–9:00 PM — don’t forget your $1 bills!
📝 Silent & Dessert Auctions will run throughout the evening as well.
🎟️ $15 General Admission
Includes a full spaghetti dinner (spaghetti, salad, green beans, garlic bread, cookie, and beverage) and admission to all auctions.
Tickets available online or at the door — but dinner is not guaranteed for at-the-door purchases, so grab yours early at myalaskatix.com or call 907-631-8591.
💙 This event supports 250+ incredible Special Olympics Alaska Mat-Su athletes! It is only through the generosity of our community that we are able to continue providing year-round sports training and competitions.
If you would like to sponsor or donate, please contact 907-631-8591 or message us!
Come celebrate our athletes, enjoy a delicious meal, and help us continue building a stronger, more inclusive Mat-Su!

description: I just spoke with Greg and he is available to come on Friday the 27th to speak to the older kids during lunch. title: LGBTQIA2S+ Youth & Allies Summit
description:
The LGBTQIA2S+ Youth & Allies Summit is a day of connection, empowerment, and celebration for queer and gender-diverse youth across Alaska—and the allies who uplift them. Hosted by The Queen’s Guard, this gathering centers joy, safety, education, and solidarity in a space designed to honor lived experience, build community, and spark action.
Through workshops, performances, resource sharing, and affirming dialogue, participants will explore identity, advocacy, and creative resistance. Whether you’re navigating your own journey or showing up in support, the Summit offers tools, inspiration, and belonging.
This is more than an event—it’s a launchpad for leadership, healing, and unapologetic pride.

Palmer Train Depot
610 S Valley Way, Palmer, AK

title: Mat-Su Sentinel Icebreaker: A Benefit Party at the Rink!
description:
Get ready for some fun and join us for a truly one-of-a-kind, family-friendly community night at the MTA Events Center in Palmer!
This isn’t your average concert — it’s a live band + open ice skating and dance floor experience featuring Mat-Su favorites The Super Saturated Sugar Strings. Skate to live music, dance it out on an ice-free floor, eat, drink, and celebrate local community — all while supporting nonprofit local journalism.
Your ticket includes:• Live concert entry• Open ice time (skate while the band plays!)• Ice skate rentals (while supplies last)• Big Dipper Homemade Ice Cream (while supplies last)• Soft drinks courtesy of the Odom Corporation (while supplies last)
Available for purchase at the event:• Beer by Palmer Brewing Company• Food from El Señor Moose food truck• A high-stakes raffle with great prizes
Not into skating? No problem.Ice skating is totally optional. We’ll have a separate, ice-free dance floor near the band so you can dance, listen, and hang out without ever stepping on the ice.
Why we’re doing this:This is a benefit event supporting Mat-Su Sentinel, your local nonprofit newsroom. Come skate, dance, eat, drink — and help keep community news strong in the Mat-Su.
Grab your friends, bring the kids, and help us thaw out winter with a night of music, movement, and community.

MTA Events Center
1317 S Kerry Weiland Ct, Palmer, AK

title: Circles of Security Parenting | April Cohort
description:
Alaska Family Services is currently offering Circles of Security Parenting class. This is an 8-part cohort series that is recommended for parents with infants, toddlers and children in the household up to 6 years old.

COURSE: Circles of Security Parenting

CHILDREN AGE RANGE: infants and children 0 to 6 years old.

REGISTRATION: Please share your first and last name, email, and phone number (we may text if there is a schedule change).• Email: FSPP@akafs.org (Preferred)• Call: 907-745-4920

PARTICIPANT FEE: $75

# of CLASSES: 8

REQUIREMENTS:• Must complete all 8 classes and homework to receive Certificate of Completion• Permitted to make-up 1 class, otherwise you will need to join the next scheduled class series• Homework must be complete at the start of each class

OCS INVOLVEMENT?• If participation in this class is part of a case plan with the Office of Children Services, we encourage you to contact your case worker to inquire about fee coverage via OCS purchase authorization.

FOSTER PARENT?• Participation in this class may count towards your training requirements.

LEARNING OBJECTIVES:• Understand your child’s emotional world by learning to read their social and emotional ques• Support your child’s ability to successfully manage their emotions• Enhance the development of your child’s self-esteem and healthy attachment• Honor your innate wisdom and desire for your child to be secure• Recognize and manage your own triggers.

Cohort: April (10:00 AM – 12:00 PM)
Class 1 (4/2/2026) – Welcome to COSPClass 2 (4/9/2026) – Exploring our children’s needsClass 3 (4/16/2026) – “Being With” on the CircleClass 4 (4/23/2026) – “Being With” Infants on the CircleClass 5 (4/30/2026) – The Path to SecurityClass 6 (5/7/2026) – Exploring our StrugglesClass 7 (5/14/2026) – Rupture and RepairClass 8 (5/21/2026) – Summary and Celebration
1825 S. Chugach St., Palmer

description:
Join us for a presentation on raising resilient youth!

Learn ways to foster healthy growth and development in children that correlate to stronger adult mental health and overall wellness.

title: Wasilla Community Health Fair at FYZICAL
description:
New to Health Fairs?
Please review this one-pager to learn what to expect at a health fair.
We Make It Easy to Stay on Top of Your Numbers
Alaska Health Fair, Inc., a nonprofit, has been providing health education and affordable blood screenings since 1980. Each year, thousands of Alaskans take advantage of the affordable blood tests offered at health fairs. We collaborate with the largest clinical laboratory in the nation to ensure the availability of high-quality blood screenings to all Alaskans at a nonprofit price.
Brochure Screenings & Affordable Blood
Wellness the Alaskan Way
From the North Slope to Southeast, health fairs bring neighbors together to help neighbors take charge of their health through volunteering, education and preventative screenings. Our fairs unite hundreds of local medical and non-medical volunteers with health-focused organizations who share their expertise at no cost. Exhibitor space is always free at AHF events! This grassroots model of Alaskans supporting Alaskans has been building a stronger, healthier state.
Would You Like to Host a Health Fair?
Our incredible team makes hosting a health fair a breeze! We’ll provide comprehensive assistance at every stage to ensure your event’s success. Fill out this quick form, and we’ll contact you with the details.

title: The KrrBrrr
description:
Come join us for the 9th annual Krrbrrr! Spend an hour or a day–or anything in between–trail running with your closest running friends and nemeses; very low key.Distance options: Approx. 5–mile loops (with optional 2.5 mile loop); run as many as you please in the 8hr time period, until you can’t feel your limbs, or until you can’t give any more blood.Course has variety of flat, steep, rolling, wide, double-wide, and single-track trails. Slush, slop, mud, ice, poop (horse variety), thorns, blood, views, guaranteed.Newcomers: If you’re looking for a polished, hand held, catered race…you’ve come to the wrong place. This is just an excuse to run through the mud and shake the cobwebs off your winter brain. Sign-ups after March 6 are NOT guaranteed a shirt.This year the KrrBrrr is excited to announce we will be donating a portion of the race proceeds to the Mat-Su Sentinel, a nonprofit news website with free, community-based news for Mat-Su.
Jim Creek
352 N Chugach St, Palmer

title: 🌷 April 1st Saturday Vendor Market – Celebrate Spring with Local Creativity! 🌷
description:
Spring is in the air, and the April 1st Saturday Vendor Market is the perfect place to celebrate! Join us for a day filled with local talent, handmade treasures, and delicious treats — all created and shared by amazing Alaskan makers.
Explore crochet and knit items, hand-dyed yarns, and hand-sewn bags, wallets, and purses. Discover stone and gem jewelry, handcrafted pens, and unique laser-engraved gifts, plus leather goods, D&D and pride items, and embroidered creations like bags, patches, and apparel.
Enjoy the flavors of Alaska with fermented and freeze-dried foods, sourdough stuffed breads, cookies, brownies, and other baked treats. Don’t miss elderberry syrup and natural products, candles made from barrel staves, and stunning stained glass art to brighten your home.
You’ll also find polymer clay dragons, greeting cards, pet treats and toys, plus info on chiropractic and massage services and cell phone and TV options.
When you’re ready for a bite, grab a meal from one of our three local food trucks and enjoy the community spirit that makes this market special!
Shop Local • Support Makers • Celebrate Spring
350 E Dahlia Ave, Palmer

title: Mat-Su College Sumo Robotics Competition
description: The Mat-Su College Robotics Club is hosting the Mat-Su College Sumo Robotics Competition, which will take place on Saturday, April 11, 2026, at 10:00 AM in the Glenn Massay Theater. The competition welcomes all high school and college students, who may enter individually or as part of a team. Participation is free, and winners will be awarded a three-credit tuition waiver at Mat-Su College. The Robotics Competition will follow international Sumo Robotics rules. Each robot will be autonomous and not require direct human intervention. The rounds will be three minutes and are won by one robot pushing the other robot out of the sumo ring.

title: Organic Gardening: Build Your Soil's Microbe Bank
description:
This is an in-person Organic Gardening class that meets at Mat-Su College in the Snodgrass Hall Building (SGH), first floor in Room 117. This course meets for 1 day only from 12:00-1:30 p.m. Cost is $25.
Your soil may have all the minerals in the world–but they are useless if your garden lacks the microorganisms to deliver them to your plants. But how? Which ones? Snatch up this practical guidance that will have our soil “teaming’ with helpful microbes! This course meets for a one 90-mintue session. The instructor presents the material via a slideshow and with hands-on demonstrations.

Consign49 Spring 2026 Consignment Sale
description:
Who is over this crazy winter and ready for Spring!!! Consign49 is back with our one stop shop for amazing deals on a selection of over 40,000 clothing, accessories, and home decor items!
Join us this spring for our 26th semi-annual consignment pop-up, being held at Raven Hall, at the Alaska State Fairgrounds, April 16-19!
Admission and parking are FREE! No tickets or reservation times are required except for during our presales on Wednesday, April 15th!
Sale Schedule and Hours:Presales:Wednesday, April 15th: (pass required, tickets on sale mid-March)
Public Shopping:Thursday, April 16th: 9am – 8pmFriday, April 17th: 9am – 8pmSaturday, April 18th: 9am – 6pmSunday, April 19th: 9am – 5pm, many items HALF PRICE!
This upscale resale shopping experience brings you great finds on new and gently used brand name and designer clothing, accessories, shoes, home décor, furniture, and so much more!There is truly something for everyone.
Consign49 is dependent upon our amazing consignors from the local community. Not only do consignors get to earn cash for items sold, but they also get to shop presales before our pop-up opens to the public! Consignor Registration opens on February 11th at 10am! Spots are limited and expected to go quickly.
To find out more about how consigning works and how to register, go to www.consign49.com/consign
Make sure you are signed up for our email newsletter to get registration notifications, sale date reminders and more! You can sign up directly from our website at www.consign49.com from the bottom of any page.
We hope to see you there!

Raven Hall
2075 S Glenn Hwy, Palmer

title: 2026 Trail Running Film Festival & Run Expo
description:
TRFF has become a celebration for the trail running community, highlighting the spirit of adventure, perseverance, and connections to nature that make trail running a unique and thrilling experience. As we gear up for the 2026 edition, our commitment to showcasing the most compelling and diverse stories in trail running remains unwavering. Our mission is to celebrate running as a universal practice to explore our collective and individual potential and pursue our wildest dreams.
Black Wolf Endurance is proud to share these stories with you as we get ready for our own race season ahead. We hope these films will connect our community and our runners in pursuing their own adventures, conquering their own goals, and making their own great stories.
Our screening of TRFF will be held on Wednesday, April 16 at 7:00pm at the Train Depot in Palmer, Alaska.
Doors will open at 5:30pm for the Valley’s first mini Run Expo. Come visit with race directors and local running clubs/groups to see what running and volunteer opportunities are available to you here in the MatSu Valley.
Run Expo Attending Groups:
AKtive Soles
Progressive PT
NXNW Relays
MatSu Running Club
TNR Healthwise
Run Palmer
…and more.

Film is 90 minutes in length with one intermission.
Be ready for a fun night of community with some special surprises and many giveaways in store!
Palmer Train Depot  S. Valley Way Palmer

title: 5th Grade - Lunch with a Loved One

2026 Clean Air Challenge
description: Join us on May 9, 2026, at the breathtaking Talkeetna Alaskan Lodge for the Clean Air Challenge, hosted by the American Lung Association in Alaska. Pedal with purpose through Alaska’s majestic landscapes on one of four fully supported cycling routes 100, 60, 26, or 10 miles or take part in our third annual 5K Walk/Run. Whether you’re a seasoned Alaskan adventurer or simply seeking a new way to experience Talkeetna’s beauty, this event offers something for everyone. More than just a ride, it’s a mission in motion raising critical funds to fight lung disease and promote clean air for all Alaskans.

title: Fireside Writers Circle
description:
Check out our writing club: Fireside Writers Circle! This is a beginner-friendly club meant to encourage writers of any genre.
We meet the last Wednesday of the month from 7:00-8:00pm.
Meetings are limited to 12 spots; if you are interested, please sign up in the store or give us a call at (907) 745-2665 and we will add your name to the list!

title: Coffee w/ A Veteran
description:
Join us every Saturday morning and get loud at 10am for Coffee with a Veteran! It’s always an interesting series of discussions and it ends whenever the “good ‘ol boys” shovel off!
FREE coffee and locally procured donuts
Whether you’re a Veteran or not, all are invited to hang out and chat.

title: Miles for Meals on Wheels Fun Run
description:
We are excited to announce our Annual Miles for Meals Fun Run, taking place on Saturday, June 20th. Registration begins at 9:00 a.m., and the run starts at 10:00 a.m.
This special event is more than just a fun morning of walking and running it is a powerful way for our community to come together in support of the Meals on Wheels program. Every mile completed helps ensure that seniors in our community receive nutritious meals and the caring connections that mean so much.
This year, our goal is to raise $100,000 to help sustain and expand our Meals on Wheels services. With rising food and delivery costs, community support is more important than ever. The funds raised will go directly toward providing meals to seniors who depend on this vital program to remain independent and healthy in their own homes.
We invite you to participate, sponsor a runner, create a personal fundraiser, or make a direct contribution. Every donation large or small makes a meaningful difference.
Together, we can ensure that no senior goes without a warm meal and a friendly visit.
